articles.summary:
Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier said the state has expanded its emergency restrictions on products related to 7-hydroxymitragynine, or 7-OH, a compound derived from kratom.

At a news conference in Tampa, Uthmeier said he signed an emergency rule adding products and chemicals related to 7-OH to the state's banned substances list. The newly restricted products include Hydroxy, 7Tabz and 7OHMZ. Additional chemicals named in the rule include 7-hydroxymitragynine, mitragynine pseudoindoxyl,
articles.content:
Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier said the state has expanded its emergency restrictions on products related to 7-hydroxymitragynine, or 7-OH, a compound derived from kratom.

At a news conference in Tampa, Uthmeier said he signed an emergency rule adding products and chemicals related to 7-OH to the state's banned substances list. The newly restricted products include Hydroxy, 7Tabz and 7OHMZ. Additional chemicals named in the rule include 7-hydroxymitragynine, mitragynine pseudoindoxyl, 7-acetoxymitragynine, 9-hydroxycorynantheidine, 10-hydroxycorynantheidine, MGM-15 and MGM-16.

Under the rule, products may not contain more than 1 milligram of the listed chemicals per gram, and any product containing 7-OH or related compounds must contain at least 100 times more regular mitragynine by mass. The order also places the substances in Schedule I, allowing felony-level enforcement, including arrests, seizures and business shutdowns, according to the attorney general.

Agriculture Commissioner Wilton Simpson said inspectors have removed 23,000 packages of 7-OH products since August. Uthmeier said he is asking lawmakers to make the restrictions permanent by expanding Florida's new Kratom Consumer Protection Act. Industry group Holistic Alternative Recovery Trust disputed the state's rationale, with policy director Jeff Smith citing FDA data that he said showed no confirmed deaths linked to 7-OH alone and eight reported adverse events.

articles.content:
Rep. Byron Donalds said his Republican opponents for governor are not viable candidates, pointing to fundraising totals and polling as the basis for his assessment.

On "This Week in South Florida," Donalds said viability depends on voter support rather than title alone. His campaign and political committee have about $65 million available, according to the article, compared with less than $6 million for Lt. Gov. Jay Collins, less than $3 million for former House Speaker Paul Renner and about $200,000 for James Fishback.

Donalds also said recent polling shows him at about 50% support while his opponents remain in single digits. Gov. Ron DeSantis has criticized the lack of a debate among the candidates. The article also said Florida Chamber of Commerce CEO Mark Wilson was scheduled to appear with Donalds at an event Tuesday, where an endorsement was expected.

Donalds has endorsements from President Donald Trump, Sen. Rick Scott and other Florida Republican officials, according to the report. Florida's primary is scheduled in less than two months.

articles.headline: Byron Donalds says Florida property tax amendment should have had fiscal review before ballot approval
articles.summary:
Rep. Byron Donalds said Florida lawmakers should have reviewed a fiscal impact study before approving a proposed homestead exemption increase for the November ballot.

Speaking on "This Week in South Florida," Donalds said the measure advanced quickly and said the question is now before voters. State economists at the Office of Economic and Demographic Research said on June 13 that, if approved, the amendment could reduce local government revenue by about $11.86 billion a year once higher exempt
articles.content:
Rep. Byron Donalds said Florida lawmakers should have reviewed a fiscal impact study before approving a proposed homestead exemption increase for the November ballot.

Speaking on "This Week in South Florida," Donalds said the measure advanced quickly and said the question is now before voters. State economists at the Office of Economic and Demographic Research said on June 13 that, if approved, the amendment could reduce local government revenue by about $11.86 billion a year once higher exemptions are fully phased in.

The proposal would raise the homestead exemption for primary residences to $150,000 in 2027 and $250,000 in 2028, with later increases tied to the consumer price index. School taxes would remain subject to a $25,000 exemption. Newer residents would begin with a $50,000 exemption and receive the full benefit after five years. The amendment would also cap assessment increases for other property at 5% annually.

Donalds said local governments might have limited some revenue pressure in recent years by reducing millage rates as property values increased. He has also said local governments could try to offset lost revenue through non-ad valorem assessments, which can fund services such as stormwater, fire rescue, street lighting and road maintenance. The amendment would need support from at least 60% of voters to pass.

articles.headline: Green Lantern’s CGI Suit Anticipated a Broader Superhero Movie Trend
articles.deck: The 2011 film *Green Lantern* drew criticism for using a fully computer-generated suit for its title character, a creative decision that was widely seen at the time as unconvincing.
articles.summary:
The 2011 film *Green Lantern* drew criticism for using a fully computer-generated suit for its title character, a creative decision that was widely seen at the time as unconvincing. In a retrospective published 15 years later, Polygon argued that the choice anticipated a broader shift in superhero filmmaking toward digitally rendered costumes.

The article said criticism of the film focused in part on the suit’s glowing appearance, lack of physical texture and how it blended with digitally creat
articles.content:
The 2011 film *Green Lantern* drew criticism for using a fully computer-generated suit for its title character, a creative decision that was widely seen at the time as unconvincing. In a retrospective published 15 years later, Polygon argued that the choice anticipated a broader shift in superhero filmmaking toward digitally rendered costumes.

The article said criticism of the film focused in part on the suit’s glowing appearance, lack of physical texture and how it blended with digitally created backgrounds. It also noted that viewers objected to design details including the mask and boot-like elements.

Polygon placed the film in the context of a wider industry move toward CGI costuming. It cited *Iron Man* in 2008 as an early example of digital suit work used alongside practical builds, and said later films relied more heavily on fully digital costumes. The article pointed to Spider-Man’s suit in *Captain America: Civil War* in 2016 as an example of a costume rendered entirely through visual effects, and said the approach remained common in subsequent Marvel productions.

The retrospective also said some recent productions have renewed interest in practical suits, citing *Spider-Man: Brand New Day* as an example. It said debate over digital versus practical costume design continues as studios face increased scrutiny over visual effects work.

articles.content:
Recent rumors about a possible return of Bleach to Weekly Shonen Jump have renewed attention on the manga's so-called "Hell Arc," according to Polygon. The outlet reported that there is no official confirmation that a new arc is in production.

Polygon said discussion centers on the 2021 one-shot chapter No Breathes from Hell, which was published to mark the series' 20th anniversary. The chapter is set 12 years after the war with the Quincy and introduces new information about Hell within the series' cosmology.

According to Polygon, the chapter depicts an ancient ritual in Soul Society that is interrupted by attacks and the return of Szayelaporro Granz, who explains that powerful captains may also be sent to Hell after death. The chapter also suggests that the balance between Hell and other worlds has been destabilized.

Polygon reported that speculation about a continuation has increased as the final season of the Thousand-Year Blood War anime approaches its July 25 debut. The outlet said rumors remain unconfirmed.

articles.content:
An art book for Toy Story 5 includes concept material for an alternate ending centered on Jessie and her former owner Emily, according to IGN. The outlet reported that the unused idea would have shown Emily, now a grandmother, reuniting with Jessie.

IGN cited comments from director Kenna Harris, who said the concept explored a climactic sequence in which Emily introduced Jessie to her grandchild. Harris said the film ultimately took a different direction but kept Jessie's connection to Emily as a central part of the story.

According to IGN, the released version instead resolves Jessie's story by showing that Emily still remembered her and had named her daughter after the toy. The report said the ending reframes Jessie's earlier abandonment as part of Emily growing up.

IGN also cited comments from story artist Tessa Abrams, who said the scene was considered important to Jessie's character arc from the start.

IGN, citing Variety, reported that the film was produced on a $250 million budget before marketing costs. Toy Story 4 earned $1.07 billion globally in 2019.

Disclosure Day had a steeper second weekend declin
articles.content:
Toy Story 5 opened with $312 million worldwide, including $160 million in domestic sales and $152 million internationally, according to IGN. The outlet said that was the largest opening weekend of 2026 so far and the biggest debut in the Toy Story franchise without adjusting for inflation.

IGN, citing Variety, reported that the film was produced on a $250 million budget before marketing costs. Toy Story 4 earned $1.07 billion globally in 2019.

Disclosure Day had a steeper second weekend decline. IGN reported that the film fell 62% domestically, taking in $17 million in North America and $18.7 million internationally for a global total of $160.4 million.

According to Variety, as cited by IGN, Disclosure Day was made on a $115 million budget and may need about $300 million worldwide to reach profitability after theater revenue splits. IGN also reported that Obsession has reached $333.2 million globally and Backrooms has reached $301 million.

articles.content:
Dbrand has opened orders for a Portal-themed case designed for Valve's unreleased Steam Machine. The company said the product is scheduled to ship in late July.

According to IGN, the case includes rear and bottom ventilation, removable front and back panels for port access, and a cutout for the device's front LED strip. The front panel is magnetic and can be removed for cleaning.

Dbrand is offering two versions. The $129.99 "Companion Cube" edition includes themed packaging, a stand, a cleaning cloth, and a Steam Controller skin. A $99.95 "Poverty Cube" version includes the case in simpler packaging. IGN reported that the lower-priced edition is listed as shipping "eventually."

The accessory is tied to Valve's planned Steam Machine, which has not yet been released. IGN said Valve delayed the system earlier this year.

Tom Hanks said Disney could potentially reuse archived recordings of his Woody performances in future Toy Story films, though he did not confirm any plans for a sixth installment.

Speaking to Entertainment Weekly, Hanks said digital records of past dialogue could allow the studio to assemble new voice lines using existing material. He also said any additional Toy Story film would need a strong creative reason to be made.

Morgan said the ferris wheel may not have been flagged t
articles.content:
A short looping video on Rockstar Games’ official Grand Theft Auto 6 website has drawn attention from fans after viewers noticed that a lit ferris wheel in the background does not appear to reflect in the surrounding water.

IGN reported that the omission led to online jokes describing the object as a “vampire ferris wheel.” Digital Foundry’s Tom Morgan said the missing reflection may be related to the game’s ray-traced reflection system.

Morgan said the ferris wheel may not have been flagged to appear in reflections, possibly as a performance-saving measure, and added that camera distance may also have contributed. He said the issue could be adjusted before the final release.

Rockstar recently said Grand Theft Auto 6 preorders will begin on June 25.

articles.content:

articles.content:

articles.content:

articles.content:

Polygon reported that the book, due out Sept. 22, is Grace's adult fantasy debut and the first installment in a planned trilogy. The story is set in a world where angels are fighting demons tied to the seven deadly sins.

Grace said she wanted to build a large setting with multiple cultures, belief systems and forms of magic. Polygon's feature included an excerpt from chapter seven introducing the angel Uriel as he tracks and kills a demon at a banquet before secretly burying her rather than surrendering the body for harvesting.

The novel was reported to have drawn strong publisher interest before release.

Destiny 2 players have been using an exploit that allows repeated stacking of artifact mods, producing much higher damage than intended against bosses.

GameSpot reported that the exploit can be activated through a competitive PvP lobby, where players can assign the same artifact mod multiple times. One frequently used combination involves stacking Thunderous Retort with the Geomag Stabilizers exotic armor to greatly increase Arc Super damage.

articles.content:
Destiny 2 players have been using an exploit that allows repeated stacking of artifact mods, producing much higher damage than intended against bosses.

GameSpot reported that the exploit can be activated through a competitive PvP lobby, where players can assign the same artifact mod multiple times. One frequently used combination involves stacking Thunderous Retort with the Geomag Stabilizers exotic armor to greatly increase Arc Super damage.

Bungie said on social media that it is planning a fix and indicated it could arrive the following week. The studio said it had found the videos of players using the exploit "quite hilarious" and, for the moment, was allowing players to continue using it.

The report said the bug is expected to be addressed in a weekly update.

articles.content:
Microsoft has clarified that local split-screen co-op for Halo: Campaign Evolved on PlayStation 5 will not require two PlayStation Plus subscriptions.

GameSpot reported that Halo Studios said each local player will need a PlayStation account, but only the earlier community answer incorrectly stated that both accounts also needed PlayStation Plus. Microsoft said the requirement to link accounts to Microsoft services remains in place to support features such as cross-progression.

The game is scheduled for release on July 28 for PC, Xbox Series X|S and PlayStation 5. It is a remake of the original Halo and includes updated graphics, new prequel missions and other quality-of-life changes.

The clarification followed confusion among players after an earlier statement from a community manager.

Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot died in a plane crash on June 19 in La Baule, western France. He was 69.

GameSpot, citing Ubisoft and other reports, said Guillemot was one of two people killed in the crash of a twin-engine Cessna 421 while approaching the city's airport. The other person was identified by the Associated Press as a flight instructor.

Ubisoft said it was deeply saddened by Guillemot's death and extended condolences to his family and loved ones. Claude Guillemot co-founded Ub
articles.content:
Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot died in a plane crash on June 19 in La Baule, western France. He was 69.

GameSpot, citing Ubisoft and other reports, said Guillemot was one of two people killed in the crash of a twin-engine Cessna 421 while approaching the city's airport. The other person was identified by the Associated Press as a flight instructor.

Ubisoft said it was deeply saddened by Guillemot's death and extended condolences to his family and loved ones. Claude Guillemot co-founded Ubisoft in 1986 with his four brothers, including current chief executive Yves Guillemot.

He also served on Ubisoft's board and was chief executive of Guillemot Corporation, which makes gaming accessories and owns the Thrustmaster brand.

articles.content:

According to the review, the episode shows Hirose dealing with a breakup and opening up to Nakamura, which gives the series one of its clearer moments of emotional reciprocity. The review says the finale has Nakamura accept that his connection with Hirose may take a less central place in his life as they move up a grade.

The piece argues that the ending offers a relatively healthy conclusion to Nakamura's arc, while also saying the series was uneven in tone and did not always build its central relationship consistently. It notes a possible setup for a second season but says it is unclear how much source material remains.

The anime is streaming on Crunchyroll.

IGN reported that the game describes itself as a roughly 10-minute experience in which players walk a red carpet and add their name to a wall that later visitors can see. Its store page says the high price is intentional.

articles.content:
A Steam title called Congratulations On Your Purchase is being sold for $999.99 and has prompted debate among users over whether it belongs on Valve's storefront.

IGN reported that the game describes itself as a roughly 10-minute experience in which players walk a red carpet and add their name to a wall that later visitors can see. Its store page says the high price is intentional.

According to IGN, the listed developer and publisher have no other games on Steam. The page also discloses that generative AI was used to create store artwork, while stating that the game itself does not generate AI content.

Some users on the Steam forum called the title a scam, while others argued that the page is transparent about what is being sold. IGN said the game was launched on May 28 and remained on the platform as of publication.

articles.headline: Shinji Mikami Says Developers Must Make Games Worth Playing Even After Streams
articles.deck: Resident Evil creator Shinji Mikami said game developers are responsible for making titles that people still want to play themselves even if they have already watched a full stream.
articles.summary:
Resident Evil creator Shinji Mikami said game developers are responsible for making titles that people still want to play themselves even if they have already watched a full stream.

IGN, citing Nikkan Sports, reported that Mikami made the comment in response to a question from comedian and streamer Eiko Kano about spoilers and livestreams. Mikami said that if viewers feel fully satisfied just by watching a game to the end, then that reflects the value the game offers, and that creators should a
articles.content:
Resident Evil creator Shinji Mikami said game developers are responsible for making titles that people still want to play themselves even if they have already watched a full stream.

IGN, citing Nikkan Sports, reported that Mikami made the comment in response to a question from comedian and streamer Eiko Kano about spoilers and livestreams. Mikami said that if viewers feel fully satisfied just by watching a game to the end, then that reflects the value the game offers, and that creators should aim higher.

Kano said he had worried about how developers viewed streaming, but said Mikami's response reassured him. IGN also reported that Dragon Quest creator Yuji Horii gave Kano a similar answer.

Mikami is now working at Unbound, the studio later acquired by Stellar Blade developer Shift Up.

articles.content:
A newly released Steam title called Congratulations On Your Purchase is listed for $1,000 and presents itself as a short interactive experience rather than a conventional video game.

According to GameSpot, the store page describes the title as a first-person experience set in a palace with a red carpet, chandelier and velvet rope barrier. The listing says there is no combat, no quests and no progression systems.

The developer, Minimal Viable Prestige, states in the description that the price is intentional and part of the concept. Steam's refund policy would still allow returns if a buyer has owned the game for less than two weeks and played it for under two hours.

GameSpot compared the title's price with high-cost collector's editions and promotional releases that have appeared in the industry in earlier years.

According to IGN, Frost told The Times that he is trying to honor Robbie Coltrane's performance while also bringing added depth to the character through the longer television format. He said each season gives him more time to explore Hagrid than the films did.

Frost said his version of the character will have a different accent
articles.content:
Actor Nick Frost said some viewers may not embrace his portrayal of Hagrid in HBO's new Harry Potter television adaptation, but he said that response is to be expected.

According to IGN, Frost told The Times that he is trying to honor Robbie Coltrane's performance while also bringing added depth to the character through the longer television format. He said each season gives him more time to explore Hagrid than the films did.

Frost said his version of the character will have a different accent and draw on traits he associates with childlike warmth and restraint. He added that some audience members may prefer the earlier version of the character, and that he accepts that reaction.

IGN also reported that writing is underway on the second season and filming is expected to begin later this year.

articles.summary:
Mothership Games says its upcoming trading card game Cataclysm Arcade is designed so each player can begin with a single booster pack while still supporting broader formats such as constructed play and draft.

In an interview with IGN, co-founder Brian David-Marshall said the game has been in development for nearly two years and was built around making a booster pack playable on its own, while also supporting multiplayer social play. He said the design uses generic resources, level-based card de
articles.content:
Mothership Games says its upcoming trading card game Cataclysm Arcade is designed so each player can begin with a single booster pack while still supporting broader formats such as constructed play and draft.

In an interview with IGN, co-founder Brian David-Marshall said the game has been in development for nearly two years and was built around making a booster pack playable on its own, while also supporting multiplayer social play. He said the design uses generic resources, level-based card deployment and pack collation tied to a featured boss so cards in a pack work together.

David-Marshall said the team also wanted to emphasize original comic art rather than relying on generative imagery. The game's Kickstarter campaign is scheduled to run through July 8, 2026, with delivery expected in the fourth quarter of 2026.

The developers said organized play support is planned during Kickstarter fulfillment.

articles.content:
Anime News Network's review of episode 13 of Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring says the installment focuses largely on the Autumn and Summer characters as the effort to rescue Nadeshiko reaches a climax.

According to the review, the episode centers on parallel battles and a major turn involving Ayame and Ruri, while giving less attention to the series' Spring leads. The review says those developments provide some of the episode's strongest emotional material.

articles.content:
Actor Deborah Ann Woll said the planned God of War title centered on Laufey had been in development discussions since at least 2018.

articles.headline: Battlefield 6 Developer Details Roadmap and Feedback Changes
articles.deck: A Battlefield 6 developer told IGN that planned seasonal updates often begin about a year before release and are adjusted as player feedback comes in after launch.
articles.summary:
A Battlefield 6 developer told IGN that planned seasonal updates often begin about a year before release and are adjusted as player feedback comes in after launch.

In an interview with IGN, Ariel Giovannetti, seasonal and competitive creative lead at Battlefield Studios' Motive team, said some changes can be made quickly while broader adjustments require testing across the game's larger systems. She said the studio has focused recent work on requests including larger maps, improved player visib
articles.content:
A Battlefield 6 developer told IGN that planned seasonal updates often begin about a year before release and are adjusted as player feedback comes in after launch.

In an interview with IGN, Ariel Giovannetti, seasonal and competitive creative lead at Battlefield Studios' Motive team, said some changes can be made quickly while broader adjustments require testing across the game's larger systems. She said the studio has focused recent work on requests including larger maps, improved player visibility and revised versions of earlier Battlefield locations.

Giovannetti said the current season includes updated takes on Golmud Railway and Grand Bazaar, now presented as Railway to Golmud and Cairo Bazaar. She also said the studio plans to continue experimenting with factors such as time of day, weather and community-created experiences through Battlefield Portal.

The interview described the current roadmap as part of a broader effort to show players how feedback is being incorporated into the game's ongoing development.

articles.content:
Anime News Network's review of episode 11 of Ascendance of a Bookworm Part 3 says the episode resolves the previous week's magical confrontation before shifting back to Rosemyne's ongoing political and printing-related work.

According to the review, the episode shows Ferdinand helping address the fallout from a corrupted mana stone, leaving Rosemyne unable to use the material she had sought and delaying that effort by a year. The episode then turns to documents tied to past corruption in the temple and to Rosemyne's work with craftspeople on printing technology.

The review says the episode also adds family and political context involving Sylvester, Ferdinand, and Wilfried. It describes the installment as narratively significant but argues that the abrupt move from a high-stakes conflict back to routine developments creates pacing problems.

The episode is streaming on Crunchyroll.

articles.headline: CD Projekt Executive Says Some Trust Was Lost After Cyberpunk 2077 Launch

articles.summary:
CD Projekt joint CEO Michał Nowakowski said some players may not have fully regained confidence in the company following the troubled December 2020 launch of Cyberpunk 2077.

Speaking to Edge, as cited by GamesRadar, Nowakowski said the launch was difficult for the studio and that some people may have lost trust in the developer for the long term. Cyberpunk 2077 was released with major technical problems, was temporarily removed from the PlayStation Store by Sony, and prompted refund offers.

CD
articles.content:
CD Projekt joint CEO Michał Nowakowski said some players may not have fully regained confidence in the company following the troubled December 2020 launch of Cyberpunk 2077.

Speaking to Edge, as cited by GamesRadar, Nowakowski said the launch was difficult for the studio and that some people may have lost trust in the developer for the long term. Cyberpunk 2077 was released with major technical problems, was temporarily removed from the PlayStation Store by Sony, and prompted refund offers.

CD Projekt Red later continued updating the game, which the company says has sold more than 35 million copies. Nowakowski said he hopes future releases, including The Witcher 4, will help rebuild confidence among players.

CD Projekt Red is also developing a remake of the original Witcher, a new intellectual property, and other projects.

articles.summary:
Google has invested $75 million in film studio A24 as part of an AI research partnership, according to The Wall Street Journal and a statement from Google. The companies said the collaboration will focus on research and the development of tools for filmmakers across multiple projects.

Google said the partnership will involve Google DeepMind researchers and A24 filmmakers working together on new creative workflows. The company added that the investment does not give Google access to A24's conten
articles.content:
Google has invested $75 million in film studio A24 as part of an AI research partnership, according to The Wall Street Journal and a statement from Google. The companies said the collaboration will focus on research and the development of tools for filmmakers across multiple projects.

Google said the partnership will involve Google DeepMind researchers and A24 filmmakers working together on new creative workflows. The company added that the investment does not give Google access to A24's content library or data.

A24 is producing planned film adaptations of Elden Ring and Death Stranding. The announcement did not say the partnership's tools would be used on those projects.

The deal comes as AI remains a contested issue in film and games, where unions and performers have pushed for safeguards on how the technology is used.

articles.content:
Epic Games' *Fortnite* is set to add five new Sprite types in its next update on Thursday, June 25, according to IGN, after the game's core battle royale modes recorded a recent increase in player activity.

IGN reported that more than 1.2 million players were active on Saturday afternoon during two limited-time "Power Hour" events that increased rare Sprite spawns. The article said the new mechanics involve collecting, leveling and extracting Sprites for future use.

According to IGN, the new Sprite types are Striker Sprite, Fishy Sprite, Aura Sprite, Boss Sprite and Grim Reaper Sprite. The report also said Epic told IGN it is acting against attempts to sell rare Sprites on eBay because doing so violates the game's rules.

articles.content:
A Polygon guide published after the season 3 premiere of *House of the Dragon* lists the major dragons in the series, their riders, and the factions they support in the Targaryen civil war.

The guide says Vhagar remains the Greens' most significant dragon, while Rhaenyra's side has expanded its strength by adding new dragonriders, including common-born "dragonseeds," to previously riderless dragons on Dragonstone. It also tracks dragons and riders that have died in earlier battles, including Lucerys Velaryon and Arrax, and Rhaenys Targaryen and Meleys.

Polygon says the season 3 premiere further changed the balance of power by advancing the Battle of the Gullet and adding developments involving dragons such as Vermax, Moondancer and Sheepstealer.

articles.content:
Anime Limited's All the Anime account on X said the new *The Ghost in the Shell* television anime from Science SARU has announced its English dub cast.

The announced cast includes Suzie Yeung as Motoko Kusanagi, SungWon Cho as Daisuke Aramaki, Bill Butts as Batou, and Nick Apostolides as Togusa, along with additional cast members in supporting roles. The first two dubbed episodes are scheduled to screen in the United Kingdom on June 26.

Anime News Network reported that the series will premiere on Japanese television on July 7 and will stream on Prime Video in more than 240 countries and regions, excluding mainland China, Russia and Vietnam. The first two episodes are also scheduled to screen at Anime Expo on July 4, followed by a panel with staff members including director Mokochan and character designer Shuhei Handa.

articles.content:

Infold described Valko as the chairman of EonCore Tech and the head of a werewolf clan. The publisher said the update will also introduce a new romance line and additional companions for players to unlock.

The episode presents the devices as sentient artificial lifeforms from space that petrify organic life in what they describe as an effort to reduce death.

articles.content:
Episode 36 of *Dr. Stone: Science Future* centers on the series' explanation for the petrification of humanity and the origin of the Medusa devices. The episode presents the devices as sentient artificial lifeforms from space that petrify organic life in what they describe as an effort to reduce death.

The review says the episode frames the Medusas as acting without a separate controller and describes their stated goal as preserving life while relying on revived humans to restore them and supply diamond batteries. It also notes that intelligence appears to play a role in emerging from petrification, citing Senku and Xeno as early examples.

According to the review, the episode relies heavily on exposition and flashbacks as it explains the story's remaining major mysteries. The piece says the installment may divide viewers because it leans further into fantasy elements, while also highlighting a scene in which a Medusa imitates Senku's voice and briefly disrupts the mission.

articles.content:
A World of Warcraft player has created a private Wrath of the Lich King-era server populated by 1,800 AI-powered bot characters, according to GameSpot.

The report cited a post on the wowservers subreddit from user Mr-Nilsson_85, who described the project as a proof of concept for a largely single-player version of the MMORPG. The bots reportedly complete quests, level professions, fight enemies, and improve gear in ways meant to resemble human players.

GameSpot said the bots’ chat system is powered by DeepSeek AI, allowing them to respond to one another and to players, though their dialogue is not connected to gameplay scripts. The report also noted mixed reactions from Reddit users and referenced Blizzard’s recent legal action against private server operators.

articles.content:
Annecy is positioned less as a fan convention and more as a global animation industry forum, according to an Anime News Network column about the Annecy International Animation Festival.

The article said Annecy and its MIFA market serve producers, directors, rights holders, and studio executives, while events such as Anime Expo and Japan Expo are more consumer-facing showcases for existing titles and announcements. It described Annecy as a place where future animation projects are pitched, financed, and developed.

The piece also highlighted Annecy’s importance to Japanese animation, citing past recognition for filmmakers including Hayao Miyazaki, Isao Takahata, and Masaaki Yuasa, along with current participation by companies such as Toei Animation and Crunchyroll.

articles.content:
Early reviews of Valve’s Steam Machine describe it as an accessible living room PC but raise questions about performance relative to its price, according to Polygon.

The outlet summarized commentary from IGN, Digital Foundry, PC Gamer, The Verge, and Eurogamer. Benchmark results cited in the report included Cyberpunk 2077 averaging 64 frames per second at 1440p and lower results in newer games such as Crimson Desert.

Polygon said several reviewers praised the system’s plug-and-play approach and Linux-based flexibility, while others argued similarly priced gaming PCs offer stronger performance. The Steam Machine starts at $1,049, with higher-priced models reaching $1,428.

articles.content:
A fan-made PC recreation of The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time has been halted after roughly a decade of development, according to GameSpot.

articles.headline: Valve Opens Steam Machine Preorders With Waitlist and Four Configurations

articles.summary:
Valve has opened a preorder process for the Steam Machine built around an initial waitlist, according to Polygon. Customers can sign up through June 25 at 10 a.m. PDT, after which Valve will randomize the first group of reservations.

To qualify, users must have a Steam account in good standing and must have made at least one purchase before April 27, 2026. Valve said customers selected for a reservation will receive an email and have 72 hours to complete a purchase when stock becomes available.
articles.content:
Valve has opened a preorder process for the Steam Machine built around an initial waitlist, according to Polygon. Customers can sign up through June 25 at 10 a.m. PDT, after which Valve will randomize the first group of reservations.

To qualify, users must have a Steam account in good standing and must have made at least one purchase before April 27, 2026. Valve said customers selected for a reservation will receive an email and have 72 hours to complete a purchase when stock becomes available.

Polygon reported that Valve is offering four configurations: a 512 GB model for $1,049, a 512 GB model with a controller for $1,128, a 2 TB model for $1,349, and a 2 TB model with a controller for $1,428. The company is also limiting purchases to one per household.

Polygon included Valve’s published specifications for the official Ste
articles.content:
Valve says users who do not buy its new Steam Machine can assemble a similar living room PC themselves using SteamOS 3.8, according to an updated company FAQ cited by Polygon.

The company said SteamOS can now run on user-built hardware, though Polygon reported that support currently extends only to AMD graphics processors. Valve also told The Verge it is working with Nvidia to improve compatibility with additional hardware.

Polygon included Valve’s published specifications for the official Steam Machine, including a semi-custom AMD Zen 4 CPU, a semi-custom AMD RDNA3 GPU, 16 GB of DDR5 memory, and storage options of 512 GB or 2 TB. The system also includes Wi-Fi 6E, Bluetooth 5.3, gigabit ethernet, and multiple display and USB ports.

Valve’s newly priced Steam Machine may have originally been expected to cost substantially less, according to a GameSpot report citing comments Valve engineers made to IGN and Aftermath.

The report said Valve indicated the Steam Machine’s price rose in a way similar to earlier Steam Deck increases. Based on those comments, GameSpot estimated the device may once have been targeted at roughly $750 before component costs increased.

articles.content:
Episode 12 of Daemons of the Shadow Realm shifts focus from the central siblings to broader world-building and the competing factions surrounding them, according to Anime News Network's recap.

The episode follows the Kagemori faction as it investigates how kidnappers entered its compound through what should have been a nonexistent gate. It also shows Asuma meeting an uncle tied to a splinter faction, suggesting disagreements within the group over how to handle the twins.

The episode also moves to Higashi Village, where it is revealed that some villagers knew Yuru would need to die to obtain Seal and that the bandits he killed were part of an internal plot. The story ends with Ivan entering Higashi Village by hiding inside Yama's body, a development that raises questions about the village's defenses and its role in the wider conflict.

articles.content:
Steam Next Fest concluded with a range of game demos drawing attention ahead of planned releases later in 2026 and beyond. Polygon highlighted seven titles after testing demos during the event.

The list included About Fishing, a horror game set in rural Japan that combines a murder mystery with fishing mechanics; Good Heavens!, a survival game that emphasizes humor; and Empulse, a fast-paced shooter from 1047 Games built around movement and mech combat. Empulse is scheduled to launch on June 24.

Other titles on the list were Over The Hill, an open-world driving game focused on exploration; Blood Dungeon, a roguelike from the creators of Nidhogg; No Such Place, an isometric co-op game centered on anomalous zones and weapon customization; and Virtue and a Sledgehammer, an action game about uncovering a story tied to a childhood town. Most of the games cited are scheduled for release later this year, while Blood Dungeon remains listed as coming soon.

articles.headline: Reported Schwab-Cboe Effort Could Bring Prediction-Style Contracts to Brokerage Accounts

articles.summary:
Charles Schwab is reportedly working with Cboe on products tied to whether the S&P 500 closes above or below specified levels, according to a June 20 Wall Street Journal report cited by CryptoSlate. The proposal would adapt prediction-style trading into regulated listed options infrastructure rather than crypto-native markets.

CryptoSlate said Cboe has outlined a Mini-SPX product using a traditional options wrapper, cash settlement and fixed-return outcomes, with supporting materials including
articles.content:
Charles Schwab is reportedly working with Cboe on products tied to whether the S&P 500 closes above or below specified levels, according to a June 20 Wall Street Journal report cited by CryptoSlate. The proposal would adapt prediction-style trading into regulated listed options infrastructure rather than crypto-native markets.

CryptoSlate said Cboe has outlined a Mini-SPX product using a traditional options wrapper, cash settlement and fixed-return outcomes, with supporting materials including listing and fee documents published in June. Customer availability through Schwab had not been confirmed at the time of publication, and a broader Cboe proposal remained under regulatory review into July 2026, according to the article.

The report said brokerage-based contracts could appeal to retail investors by allowing outcome-based trades within existing brokerage accounts, without the wallet setup and settlement mechanisms used on some crypto platforms. It contrasted that model with crypto-native venues such as Polymarket, which use tokenized outcome shares and blockchain-based market infrastructure.

If launched, the products could expand competition in prediction-style markets by moving some financial-event trading into traditional brokerage channels. CryptoSlate said the outcome may depend on regulatory timing, product scope, fees and whether liquidity develops after launch.

articles.content:
Spot XRP exchange-traded funds recorded a sixth straight week of inflows through mid-June, while U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs posted large outflows over the same period. The divergence has drawn attention because money continued to enter selected altcoin products even as investors reduced exposure to larger crypto funds.

The article said one recent week brought about $67.6 million into XRP funds, while Bitcoin ETFs saw roughly $1.44 billion in outflows. Since launching in November 2025, XRP funds have accumulated about $1.44 billion, a total that remains far smaller than the cumulative flows into Bitcoin ETFs since their 2024 debut.

Analysts cited several factors behind the interest in XRP, including its regulatory position after Ripple's legal case with the SEC, expectations tied to proposed U.S. legislation, and institutional interest in its payments-related use case. At the same time, the article noted that the scale of the XRP inflows remains limited relative to the size of Bitcoin funds and the broader crypto market.

That difference in scale may help explain why XRP inflows have not translated into immediate price gains. The reported trend points to sustained institutional interest in XRP, but the article said it should not be read on its own as a near-term forecast for price direction.

articles.headline: What MEV Means for Blockchain Users and Markets
articles.deck: Maximal extractable value, or MEV, refers to profit earned by parties that control or influence the order of transactions in a blockchain block.
articles.summary:
Maximal extractable value, or MEV, refers to profit earned by parties that control or influence the order of transactions in a blockchain block. That can include placing transactions ahead of, behind or around other pending trades to capture arbitrage, liquidation opportunities or other gains.

The concept emerged from the way public blockchains process transactions. On networks where pending transactions are visible before confirmation, automated traders can monitor the queue and submit competi
articles.content:
Maximal extractable value, or MEV, refers to profit earned by parties that control or influence the order of transactions in a blockchain block. That can include placing transactions ahead of, behind or around other pending trades to capture arbitrage, liquidation opportunities or other gains.

The concept emerged from the way public blockchains process transactions. On networks where pending transactions are visible before confirmation, automated traders can monitor the queue and submit competing transactions designed to benefit from ordering. Researchers and market participants generally distinguish between MEV tied to functions such as arbitrage and liquidations, and practices such as sandwich attacks that can worsen prices for ordinary users.

A specialized market has developed around the activity. Searchers identify opportunities, builders assemble blocks designed to maximize value, and validators or other block producers choose among those blocks. Tools such as Flashbots and MEV-Boost were developed to move that competition into a more structured process and reduce the fee spikes associated with earlier bidding wars.

Developers and traders have also built defenses, including private transaction routing and trading systems that batch orders or reduce exposure to public transaction queues. While analysts generally view MEV as a persistent feature of public blockchains, current efforts focus on limiting harmful forms of extraction and improving how the resulting value is distributed.

articles.content:
Solana rose over three days after Morgan Stanley amended its proposed spot Solana ETF filing, adding a 0.14% sponsor fee and naming Figment and Coinbase Canada as staking providers. SOL climbed from about $68 on June 19 to an intraday high near $75 on June 22 before easing slightly.

The move followed a four-day decline from about $75.6 on June 15 to near $68, as broader risk appetite weakened across crypto markets. The rebound also coincided with activity in derivatives markets, where traders increased positions as the token moved back above short-term resistance levels.

Analysts cited the area around $75 as a key test. CoinGlass data showed a concentration of leveraged positions between roughly $74.5 and $75.5, a range that could add volatility if the price moves through it. Some analysts said technical indicators pointed to heavy resistance and the possibility of a pullback, while others pointed to signs of improving momentum on longer time frames.

Support levels near $72, $71 and $70 remain important for the current recovery. Market participants are also watching broader conditions, including U.S. dollar strength, general risk sentiment and the timing of U.S. crypto ETF approvals.

articles.summary:
Bitcoin rose above $65,000 on June 22 after the U.S. Treasury temporarily authorized certain Iranian oil sales and investors responded to signs of easing geopolitical tensions and lower energy prices.

According to the article, Bitcoin climbed more than 3.5% from an intraday low of $63,231 to a high of $65,468 before easing back toward $65,000. The Treasury said a general license would allow the production, delivery and sale of Iranian crude oil, petroleum products and petrochemicals through Aug
articles.content:
Bitcoin rose above $65,000 on June 22 after the U.S. Treasury temporarily authorized certain Iranian oil sales and investors responded to signs of easing geopolitical tensions and lower energy prices.

According to the article, Bitcoin climbed more than 3.5% from an intraday low of $63,231 to a high of $65,468 before easing back toward $65,000. The Treasury said a general license would allow the production, delivery and sale of Iranian crude oil, petroleum products and petrochemicals through Aug. 21, 2026. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent linked the move to diplomatic talks in Switzerland and said Iran had committed to free transit through the Strait of Hormuz and to permit the return of International Atomic Energy Agency inspectors.

Oil prices fell to about $74 a barrel as reports pointed to a roadmap toward a possible agreement within 60 days. The article said lower crude prices eased concerns about supply disruptions and inflation, helping improve sentiment toward risk assets.

Shipping data also suggested conditions in the Strait of Hormuz were stabilizing, with 71 confirmed vessel transits recorded between June 19 and June 21, according to Marine Traffic data cited in the report. The article said traders were watching whether Bitcoin could hold above $65,000, with technical resistance around $68,200 to $68,500 and support near $63,200.

articles.content:
BingX said daily trading volume in its TradFi Stocks product rose more than 700% over five days as users sought access to multiple asset classes through a single platform.

The company said cumulative stock trading volume exceeded $2.7 billion over the last two months and that stock index volume topped $8 billion during the same period. BingX attributed the increase to interest in companies including SpaceX, Nvidia and Samsung, along with other products it described as connecting crypto-native traders to broader market themes.

Pablo Monti, a spokesperson for BingX, said the figures reflect demand for a trading experience that combines crypto, stocks, indices, commodities and other opportunities in one account. The company also said it recently launched a $1 million stock trading promotion aimed at increasing participation in global equity markets.

The article was presented as third-party content and included a disclosure stating that neither crypto.news nor the author endorsed any product mentioned. It also said the material was not investment advice.

articles.summary:
The XRP Ledger Foundation said fixes for several software issues were included in the XRPL 3.2.0 upgrade after security firm Common Prefix identified numerical and behavioral edge cases during a formal review of the network's core software.

The collaboration, announced June 22, covers formal verification and security analysis of the XRP Ledger consensus mechanism. The foundation said the issues found in xrpld, the software used by validators, have already been addressed and deployed. Common Pre
articles.content:
The XRP Ledger Foundation said fixes for several software issues were included in the XRPL 3.2.0 upgrade after security firm Common Prefix identified numerical and behavioral edge cases during a formal review of the network's core software.

The collaboration, announced June 22, covers formal verification and security analysis of the XRP Ledger consensus mechanism. The foundation said the issues found in xrpld, the software used by validators, have already been addressed and deployed. Common Prefix will also maintain the XRPL Payment Engine specification and keep it aligned with future xrpld releases.

The review is set to expand to proposed protocol features including the Single Asset Vault proposal, known as XLS-65, and the Lending Protocol proposal, known as XLS-66. RippleX Head of Engineering J Ayo Akinyele said security requirements need to keep pace as more financial functions are built into the protocol.

The announcement came as discussion continued within the XRP community over Ripple's escrow practices and future token releases. Crypto.news cited comments from attorney Bill Morgan, who argued that Ripple should return less XRP to escrow after monthly unlocks, while other holders said larger releases could increase selling pressure if demand does not keep pace. XRP rose about 3.6% on June 22 before easing later in the session, according to the article.

articles.headline: Andy Burnham Emerges as Favorite in UK Leadership Race as Crypto Rules Advance
articles.deck: Keir Starmer's decision to step down as U.K.
articles.summary:
Keir Starmer's decision to step down as U.K. prime minister has opened a Labour leadership race as the country moves toward implementation of a new regulatory framework for crypto activities. Starmer said June 22 that he would remain in office until Labour selects a successor.

Andy Burnham, who recently returned to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election, quickly became the leading candidate after confirming his bid and receiving an endorsement from Wes Streeting, according to the a
articles.content:
Keir Starmer's decision to step down as U.K. prime minister has opened a Labour leadership race as the country moves toward implementation of a new regulatory framework for crypto activities. Starmer said June 22 that he would remain in office until Labour selects a successor.

Andy Burnham, who recently returned to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election, quickly became the leading candidate after confirming his bid and receiving an endorsement from Wes Streeting, according to the article. Labour is set to open nominations on July 9, with an uncontested process potentially ending in mid-July and a contested race lasting until September.

The next prime minister would inherit legislation approved in February that expands the regulated financial-services perimeter to cover crypto trading platforms, qualifying stablecoins, custody and other digital asset activities. The Financial Conduct Authority is still developing the accompanying rules and expects the framework to begin on Oct. 25, 2027.

Some industry participants said a Burnham government could take a more growth-oriented approach to digital assets. Freddie New, chief executive of BHODL plc and a co-founder of Bitcoin Policy UK, told CryptoSlate that the leadership change could offer an opportunity to present the sector as a source of investment and listings for London markets. The article noted, however, that the underlying legislation is already advanced and that any near-term change would more likely affect political emphasis and administrative momentum than reverse the framework outright.

articles.content:
Bank of America Global Research now expects the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates by 25 basis points in September, October and December, according to a Reuters report cited by crypto.news. The revised forecast would put the policy rate in a 4.25% to 4.50% range by year-end and marks a change from the bank's earlier view that rates would remain unchanged.

The new outlook comes before the June 24 release of the U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure. Economists surveyed ahead of the report expect headline PCE inflation to rise 0.5% month over month in May and 4.1% from a year earlier, while core PCE is expected to increase 0.3% on the month and 3.4% annually.

Other banks have also adopted more hawkish forecasts. Reuters reported that Deutsche Bank expects two rate increases, in September and December, and outlined a scenario for a possible July move. Crypto.news also cited earlier reporting that BNP Paribas expects three hikes beginning in December.

Market pricing remains mixed. Kalshi data cited in the article showed a 22% probability of a July increase, while CME FedWatch data put the likelihood of a September quarter-point hike at 51.7%. Bitcoin was trading in the $64,000 to $65,000 range as investors weighed the implications of tighter monetary policy for liquidity and demand for risk assets.

articles.headline: Crypto-Backed PACs Increase Primary Spending as CLARITY Act Talks Continue

Crypto-backed political committees have spent at least $7 million in several Democratic primary races while lawmakers continue negotiating the CLARITY Act, a bill intended to create a U.S. regulatory framework for digital assets.

Protect Progress, an affiliate of Fairshake, has spent about $5.5 million supporting Maryland state delegate Adrian Boafo in the race to succeed retiring Representative Steny Hoyer in Maryland's 5th Congressional District. The group has also directed about $1.5 million
articles.content:
Crypto-backed political committees have spent at least $7 million in several Democratic primary races while lawmakers continue negotiating the CLARITY Act, a bill intended to create a U.S. regulatory framework for digital assets.

Protect Progress, an affiliate of Fairshake, has spent about $5.5 million supporting Maryland state delegate Adrian Boafo in the race to succeed retiring Representative Steny Hoyer in Maryland's 5th Congressional District. The group has also directed about $1.5 million to the reelection campaign of Representative Ritchie Torres in New York's 15th District, while Fellowship PAC has reportedly spent about $300,000 on advertising supporting Torres.

The spending follows an earlier Fairshake-backed effort of roughly $12 million in Alabama's Republican Senate primary runoff in support of Representative Barry Moore. The campaigns illustrate the scale of crypto industry political spending as Congress debates digital asset legislation.

In Washington, lawmakers are still working through committee language, ethics provisions and safeguards against illicit finance in the CLARITY Act, according to Crypto In America, as cited in the article. White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt and Senator Bill Hagerty have said they hope progress can be made before the July 4 recess. The Digital Chamber has also organized meetings between member companies and lawmakers to build support for the bill, while Senate Agriculture Committee Chairman John Boozman has said some lawmakers still do not fully understand the legislation.

KeyBanc said SpaceX remains well positioned in launch services and cited growth opportunities tied to Starlink and artificial intelligence. The firm said, however, that much of that potential may already be reflected in the share price an
articles.content:
SpaceX shares fell more than 10% in early U.S. trading after KeyBanc began coverage with a neutral "Sector Weight" rating and no price target, according to a June 22 Barron's report. The move added to debate over the company's valuation after its public debut.

KeyBanc said SpaceX remains well positioned in launch services and cited growth opportunities tied to Starlink and artificial intelligence. The firm said, however, that much of that potential may already be reflected in the share price and described the risk-reward balance as even at current levels.

The decline followed a strong post-listing rally that had pushed the company's valuation higher. Morningstar analysts previously estimated a fair value of $63 per share, according to earlier reporting cited in the article.

Investor attention has also turned to the company's financing plans. Barron's reported that SpaceX launched its first bond offering, issuing senior unsecured notes and planning to use the proceeds primarily to repay bridge financing, with additional funds for general corporate purposes. The bond sale came shortly after the company's June 12 initial public offering, which the report said raised more than $85 billion after underwriters exercised the greenshoe option.

The purchase came after STRC fell below par and recently traded in the low $80s before recovering part of the decline.

The stock is central to Strategy's financing model because the company can issue additional preferred shares through an at-the-market program when STRC trades a
articles.content:
Strategy Chief Executive Officer Phong Le said June 22 that he bought $1 million of the company's STRC preferred stock and plans to hold the position until the shares return to their $100 par value, and possibly longer. The purchase came after STRC fell below par and recently traded in the low $80s before recovering part of the decline.

The stock is central to Strategy's financing model because the company can issue additional preferred shares through an at-the-market program when STRC trades above par and use the proceeds for Bitcoin purchases. With the shares below $100, that funding channel has become more constrained.

The company has said its Bitcoin and cash holdings exceed its outstanding debt by about $48 billion. In a recent regulatory filing, Strategy also said its U.S. dollar reserve had risen to $1.4 billion and disclosed that it raised about $335.5 million through sales of 2.71 million MSTR shares during the previous week.

Critics have continued to question the durability of the capital structure. Peter Schiff said investors could pursue legal action over the way the preferred stock was marketed, while Arca Chief Investment Officer Jeff Dorman said Strategy might eventually need to sell some Bitcoin to reduce pressure on the structure. Strategy recently said it bought 520 Bitcoin for about $35 million, bringing total holdings to 847,363 Bitcoin. The company also disclosed selling 32 Bitcoin at the end of May to help fund obligations tied to STRC dividends.

articles.headline: Oklahoma’s Power Shutoff Rate Leads U.S. as Low-Income Families Face Repeated Disconnections
articles.deck: Oklahoma recorded more household electricity disconnections for nonpayment in 2024 than any other state relative to its customer base, according to newly released federal data t...
articles.summary:
Oklahoma recorded more household electricity disconnections for nonpayment in 2024 than any other state relative to its customer base, according to newly released federal data that for the first time provides nationwide transparency on utility shutoffs.

The data shows Oklahoma utilities disconnected households 572,480 times in 2024 among about 1.8 million customers, a rate equal to roughly 30 percent of households over the year, though some customers were disconnected more than once. The next-h
articles.content:
Oklahoma recorded more household electricity disconnections for nonpayment in 2024 than any other state relative to its customer base, according to newly released federal data that for the first time provides nationwide transparency on utility shutoffs.

The data shows Oklahoma utilities disconnected households 572,480 times in 2024 among about 1.8 million customers, a rate equal to roughly 30 percent of households over the year, though some customers were disconnected more than once. The next-highest annual rate was 23 percent in Texas, while 41 states were below 10 percent.

Utilities and advocates point to a combination of lower household incomes, fewer consumer protections and the widespread use of remote shutoff meters. Oklahoma’s residential electricity prices were among the nation’s lowest as of March, but resident incomes were lower than in all but five states. The state blocks new disconnections only when temperatures are below 32 degrees or above 101 degrees, while some states impose broader seasonal bans.

Public Service Company of Oklahoma and Oklahoma Gas & Electric said they offer payment plans and assistance options, and company officials said many customers reconnect quickly after paying. PSO said about 70 percent of its disconnections come from its Power Pay program, a pay-as-you-go service used by a small share of customers, where interruptions are often brief.

Advocates for stronger protections said the high shutoff rate still shows many households are struggling to maintain essential service. The Washington Post article highlighted several Tulsa residents who lost power while trying to cover overdue balances and deposits needed to restore service.

articles.headline: Demand Shifts Toward Protein-Rich Foods as GLP-1 Use and Health Trends Reshape Grocery Buying
articles.deck: Americans are buying fewer chips, cookies and other processed snacks and more yogurt, cottage cheese and meat snacks, according to food companies, industry analysts and federal ...
articles.summary:
Americans are buying fewer chips, cookies and other processed snacks and more yogurt, cottage cheese and meat snacks, according to food companies, industry analysts and federal data, in a shift that is affecting product sales and pricing.

Analysts say the change is being driven partly by wider use of GLP-1 medications for weight loss and diabetes, along with broader interest in higher-protein and less processed diets. A Cornell University study published in December found that households with a
articles.content:
Americans are buying fewer chips, cookies and other processed snacks and more yogurt, cottage cheese and meat snacks, according to food companies, industry analysts and federal data, in a shift that is affecting product sales and pricing.

Analysts say the change is being driven partly by wider use of GLP-1 medications for weight loss and diabetes, along with broader interest in higher-protein and less processed diets. A Cornell University study published in December found that households with at least one GLP-1 user reduced grocery spending by more than 5 percent within six months of starting the drugs, including about a 10 percent drop in spending on salty snacks. The study also found increased spending on yogurt, fresh fruit, nutrition bars and jerky.

Food companies have reported stronger demand for protein-focused products. Danone said its high-protein yogurt brands continued to post double-digit growth, and Lifeway Foods reported a 37 percent year-over-year increase in sales in May. U.S. Agriculture Department data analyzed by The Washington Post showed rising production of cottage cheese and yogurt since the second half of 2022, when semaglutide and tirzepatide became more widely used.

At the same time, some large manufacturers have reduced prices or introduced new product lines. PepsiCo lowered prices for some snack brands, while General Mills cut prices on much of its North American grocery portfolio. Companies including Kraft, Starbucks, Conagra and Nestlé have also expanded protein-focused offerings.

Analysts said the shift resembles earlier long-running changes in consumer eating habits, including the low-fat era of the late 20th century. They added that GLP-1 use is not the only factor, with broader health concerns and changing attitudes toward ultra-processed foods also influencing demand.

articles.headline: Alan Greenspan, Former Federal Reserve Chair, Dies at 100

articles.summary:
Alan Greenspan, who served as chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve under four presidents, has died at 100, according to the central bank.

Greenspan led the Federal Reserve through five terms spanning the administrations of Ronald Reagan, George H.W. Bush, Bill Clinton and George W. Bush. He was one of the most prominent figures in U.S. monetary policy during that period.

In a statement announcing his death on Monday, the Federal Reserve said Greenspan helped establish the institution’s credibilit
articles.content:
Alan Greenspan, who served as chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve under four presidents, has died at 100, according to the central bank.

Greenspan led the Federal Reserve through five terms spanning the administrations of Ronald Reagan, George H.W. Bush, Bill Clinton and George W. Bush. He was one of the most prominent figures in U.S. monetary policy during that period.

In a statement announcing his death on Monday, the Federal Reserve said Greenspan helped establish the institution’s credibility.

He remained a central figure in economic policy discussions long after leaving office, with his record continuing to be studied and debated by policymakers and economists.

articles.headline: Clive Davis, Record Executive Who Led Columbia and Arista, Dies at 94
articles.deck: Clive Davis, the record executive who led Columbia Records, built Arista Records into a major label and helped shape the careers of artists including Whitney Houston, Barry Mani...
articles.summary:
Clive Davis, the record executive who led Columbia Records, built Arista Records into a major label and helped shape the careers of artists including Whitney Houston, Barry Manilow and Alicia Keys, has died at 94, according to a statement posted by his family on June 22.

Davis spent more than five decades in the music industry after joining Columbia Records as an in-house lawyer in 1960. He later became head of the label and signed or worked with performers including Janis Joplin, Bob Dylan, Jo
articles.content:
Clive Davis, the record executive who led Columbia Records, built Arista Records into a major label and helped shape the careers of artists including Whitney Houston, Barry Manilow and Alicia Keys, has died at 94, according to a statement posted by his family on June 22.

Davis spent more than five decades in the music industry after joining Columbia Records as an in-house lawyer in 1960. He later became head of the label and signed or worked with performers including Janis Joplin, Bob Dylan, Johnny Cash, Bruce Springsteen, Aretha Franklin, Carlos Santana and Patti Smith.

After leaving Columbia in the 1970s, Davis took over Bell Records, renamed it Arista and expanded it into a leading label. During his 25 years there, Arista produced 200 Billboard No. 1 singles, according to the obituary. In 2000, after being replaced at Arista, he founded J Records with backing from BMG and later became chief creative officer at Sony Music Entertainment.

Davis was known for identifying commercial trends and developing artists across pop, rock, R&B and later hip-hop. Admirers credited him with strong market instincts and sustained influence over mainstream music. Critics said his emphasis on chart success could come at the expense of artistic considerations.

His career also included setbacks. Columbia fired him in 1973 over allegations involving company funds. Davis denied broader wrongdoing, and in 1976 he pleaded guilty to one count of tax evasion related to unreported non-business travel expenses. He later returned to senior leadership in the industry and remained active for decades.

articles.headline: ABC Asks Viewers to Comment as FCC Reviews ‘The View’ and Local Stations
articles.deck: ABC has begun airing on-air messages urging viewers to submit comments to the Federal Communications Commission as the agency reviews whether “The View” qualifies for an exempti...
articles.summary:
ABC has begun airing on-air messages urging viewers to submit comments to the Federal Communications Commission as the agency reviews whether “The View” qualifies for an exemption to equal-time requirements and examines the company’s local stations over diversity, equity and inclusion practices.

The FCC has been investigating since February whether appearances on “The View” complied with the commission’s equal-time rule for political candidates. ABC has said the program is a bona fide news prog
articles.content:
ABC has begun airing on-air messages urging viewers to submit comments to the Federal Communications Commission as the agency reviews whether “The View” qualifies for an exemption to equal-time requirements and examines the company’s local stations over diversity, equity and inclusion practices.

The FCC has been investigating since February whether appearances on “The View” complied with the commission’s equal-time rule for political candidates. ABC has said the program is a bona fide news program and therefore exempt, and it argued in a legal filing that the agency’s actions violate the First Amendment.

In April, the FCC also ordered an early review of ABC’s eight local stations over DEI practices. ABC’s new television spots ask viewers to comment before a July 6 deadline. One ad says the FCC wants to control who can appear on “The View,” while another asks viewers in local markets to support their stations.

An FCC spokesperson said Disney was running a campaign that misled viewers about the law. The dispute comes as FCC Chairman Brendan Carr has pursued reviews involving several media companies and broadcast transactions. Carr has also publicly criticized ABC and late-night host Jimmy Kimmel.

The FCC said its review is based on regulatory questions. ABC has framed the investigations as government pressure on its programming and stations.
